question: Will "LOVE ON DIGITAL - Destin Conrad" win Best Progressive R&B Album at the 68th annual GRAMMY Awards?
description:
The GRAMMY Awards are presented annually by the Recording Academy. For the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards, nominations are scheduled for November 7, 2025, and the ceremony for February 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed album that wins Best Progressive R&B Album at the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or in case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed album that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the television broadcast of the awards and the Grammy website (https://www.grammy.com/); however, other credible reporting may be used.

The GRAMMY Awards are presented annually by the Recording Academy. For the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards, nominations are scheduled for November 7, 2025, and the ceremony for February 1, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed track that wins Best Rap Song at the 68th Annual GRAMMY Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or in case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed track that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the television broadcast of the awards and the Grammy website (https://www.grammy.com/); however, other credible reporting may be used.

Aaron Bolton, Dwayne Carter, Jr., Dudley Alexander Duverne, Tyler Okonma, Janae Wherry, Gloria Woods & Rex Zamor, songwriters

question: Will USD reach 1.5M Iranian rials by January 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the finalized USD exchange rate on Bonbast reaches or exceeds 150,000 Iranian toman (equivalent to 1,500,000 Iranian rials) at any point between market creation and January 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve according to the daily finalized free-market USD exchange rate as displayed on Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd), which publishes prices in Iranian toman, where 1 Iranian toman equals 10 Iranian rials (IRR).

A daily figure will be considered finalized once the following day’s figure is released.

Revisions or corrections to daily figures indicating a qualifying exchange rate will be considered only if they occur before all relevant figures for this market have been finalized.

The resolution source for this market will be Bonbast (https://www.bonbast.com/graph/usd). Resolution will occur once the January 31, 2026, exchange rate data is finalized. If the resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, another resolution source will be chosen.

question: Pam Bondi out as Attorney General by January 31?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Pam Bondi ceases to be the US Attorney General for any period of time between market creation and January 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Bondi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the Trump administration, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down on January 15?
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT Jan 14 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is lower than the final "Close" price for the Jan 15 '26 12:00 ET candle.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the "Close" price for the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT Jan 14 '26 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) is higher than the final "Close" price for the Jan 15 '26 12:00 ET candle.

If the final "Close" price for both of these candles is exactly equal on Binance, this market will resolve 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Aurora Türkiye vs Team Spirit (BO3)
description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Aurora Türkiye and Team Spirit in the World Championship M7 Group Stage, scheduled for January 15 at 2:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Aurora Türkiye" if Aurora Türkiye win the match against Team Spirit.

This market will resolve to "Team Spirit" if Team Spirit win the match against Aurora Türkiye.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page.
groupItemTitle: Aurora Gaming PH vs Team Spirit - World Championship M7 Group Stage

question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Team Liquid PH vs Yangon Galacticos (BO3)
description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Team Liquid PH and Yangon Galacticos in the World Championship M7 Group Stage, scheduled for January 15 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Liquid PH" if Team Liquid PH win the match against Yangon Galacticos.

This market will resolve to "Yangon Galacticos" if Yangon Galacticos win the match against Team Liquid PH.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page.

question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Team Falcons vs Aurora Gaming (BO3)
description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Team Falcons and Aurora Gaming in the World Championship M7 Group Stage, scheduled for January 15 at 6:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Team Falcons" if Team Falcons win the match against Aurora Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Aurora Gaming" if Aurora Gaming win the match against Team Falcons.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

description:

question: Australian Open Men's: Nishesh Basavareddy vs Sebastian Ofner
description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Nishesh Basavareddy and Sebastian Ofner in the Australian Open, Qualification ATP, scheduled for January 13 at 6:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Basavareddy' if Nishesh Basavareddy advances against Sebastian Ofner.

This market will resolve to 'Ofner' if Sebastian Ofner advances against Nishesh Basavareddy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

description:

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - January 15, 2PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

This market refers on the tennis match between Maria Lourdes Carle and Yue Yuan in the Australian Open, Qualification WTA, scheduled for January 13 at 10:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Carle' if Maria Lourdes Carle advances against Yue Yuan.

This market will resolve to 'Yuan' if Yue Yuan advances against Maria Lourdes Carle.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.
